visual.notes: Green to yellow mottling (blotchiness) of younger leaves in the upper canopy, with possible puckering, distortion, and stunting; pods can be misshapen and mottled and reduced in size, and seed may appear smoky or have seed coat mottling (brown or black depending on hilum color).

Infected plants may have green stems after the pods have matured. Seed from infected plants may appear smoky in color or have reduced size and quality due to seed coat mottling which will be brown or black depending on hilum color.

notes.value: Timber rot causes loss of plant vigor, followed by wilting and eventual death of the plant, with stem lesions showing hollow pith and white moldy growth.
notes.quote: Timber rot causes loss of plant vigor, followed by wilting and eventual death of the plant. Lesions on the stem show hollow pith when cut open, and white moldy growth is usually visible at the edges of the lesions.
